gpt55_review_summary: GPT-5.5 reviewed the vLLM smoke ownership inversion and found legacy execution, direct-helper monkeypatch, Databricks import-boundary, and typed-signature compatibility issues. The issues were fixed by restoring the legacy __main__ block, adding monkeypatch-aware typed helper wrappers, importing smoke defaults from document_kv_cache.vllm_smoke in the Databricks smoke job, and preserving concrete wrapper signatures. Final re-review found no issues.

issues.resolution: Restored the legacy if __name__ == '__main__' block and added subprocess coverage for python -m restaurant_kv_serving.vllm_smoke --help.
issues.summary: Legacy module execution no longer ran main().

issues.resolution: Added direct legacy helper wrappers that project monkeypatched legacy globals into the document implementation while skipping default wrappers to avoid recursion.
issues.summary: Broad legacy helper exports were not monkeypatch-compatible when called directly.

issues.resolution: Changed databricks_vllm_smoke_job smoke-default imports to document_kv_cache.vllm_smoke.
issues.summary: The Databricks smoke job still imported vLLM smoke defaults through the legacy wrapper.

issues.resolution: Replaced generic helper wrappers with concrete typed signatures matching the document implementation.
issues.summary: Legacy helper wrappers erased typed public signatures in a py.typed package.

scope: legacy module execution and helper monkeypatch migration hooks
document and legacy package README migration notes
verification: poetry run pytest tests/test_vllm_smoke.py tests/test_databricks_vllm_smoke_job.py tests/test_public_package.py tests/test_project_governance.py -q

python -m py_compile src/document_kv_cache/vllm_smoke.py src/restaurant_kv_serving/vllm_smoke.py src/restaurant_kv_serving/databricks_vllm_smoke_job.py tests/test_vllm_smoke.py tests/test_databricks_vllm_smoke_job.py tests/test_public_package.py
find src tests -name '*.py' -print0 | xargs -0 python -m py_compile
GPT-5.5 review found four compatibility issues; all were fixed and final re-review returned no findings
what_changed: Moved the Databricks-friendly vLLM smoke implementation into document_kv_cache.vllm_smoke.
Turned restaurant_kv_serving.vllm_smoke into a compatibility wrapper over the document module.
Kept the public document __all__ curated while preserving the legacy wrapper's broader historical helper surface.
Preserved legacy module execution and direct-helper monkeypatch behavior with typed compatibility wrappers.
Updated the Databricks vLLM smoke job to import smoke defaults from the document-owned module.
Updated package READMEs and public-package tests to record the new implementation boundary.
why: The generalized package should own the self-contained Qwen3/vLLM smoke because it validates established serving-engine integration for arbitrary documents on the V1 target hardware path. This is a behavior-preserving migration step toward retiring restaurant-specific implementation ownership.
